title: Wire Load Model
sample: Wire Load Model | - A wire load model is an estimate of a net’s RC | parasitics based on the net’s fanout. | Example: dc_shell-t> set_wire_load_model 10x10

title: Setting Wire Load Mode
sample: Setting Wire Load Mode | dc_shell-t> set_wire_load_mode <top|enclosed|segmented>

title: Hierarchical Wire Load Models
sample: Hierarchical Wire Load Models | - DC supports three modes for determining which wire | load model to use for nets that cross hierarchical | boundaries:

title: Hierarchical Wire Load Models
sample: Hierarchical Wire Load Models | - Enclose: DC uses the wire load model of the smallest design that | fully encloseds the net. If the design enclosing the net has no wire load | model, the tool traverses the design hierearchy upward until it finds a wire

title: Compile is the “art” of synthesis
sample: Compile is the “art” of synthesis | - Logical level optimization | - flatten : remove structure | - structure: minimize generic logic

title: Logical Level Optimization
sample: Logical Level Optimization | - Operate with boolean representation of circuit . | - Has a global effect on the overall area/speed | characteristic of a design.

title: Structure
sample: Structure | - Factors out common sub-expression as | intermediate variable | - Useful for speed optimization as well as area

title: Flatten
sample: Flatten | - Flatten is default OFF | - Remove all intermediate variable | - Result a two-level sum-of-product form

title: Gate Level Optimization
sample: Gate Level Optimization | - Select components to meet timing, design rule | & area goals specified for the circuit. | - Has a local effect on the area/speed

sample: Combinational Mapping | - Mapping rearranges | components, | combining and re-

title: Path Delay Calculation
sample: Path Delay Calculation | - To calculate total delay, each path is broken into timing arcs. | - Each timing arc contributes either a net delay or cell delay | - All the net and cell timing arcs along the path are added

title: Setup and Hold Time Check
sample: Setup and Hold Time Check | - Setup Time: The length of time that data must stabilize | before the clock transition. | The maximum data path is used to determine if setup

title: Clock Tree Modeling
sample: Clock Tree Modeling | - Two parameter to model: | - Specify clock network latency | - set_clock_latency –rise tr –fall tf [get_ports TCK]

title: Clock Tree Modeling Example
sample: Clock Tree Modeling Example | - create_clock –period 10 –waveform {0 5} [get_ports TCK] | - set_clock_latency –rise 1 –fall 2 [get_ ports TCK] | - set_clock_uncertainty –rise 0.8 –fall 0.5 [get_ ports TCK]

title: Effect of Clock Tree Modeling on Setup
sample: Effect of Clock Tree Modeling on Setup | Time | - Assumed library (Flip Flop) setup time requirement = 1ns | create_clock –period 10 –waveform {0 5} [get_ports TCK]

title: Effect of Clock Tree Modeling on Hold
sample: Effect of Clock Tree Modeling on Hold | Time | - Assumed library (Flip Flop) hold time requirement = 1ns | create_clock –period 10 –waveform {0 5} [get_ports TCK]

title: Modeling Source Latency
sample: Modeling Source Latency | - Source latency is the propagation time from the actual clock | origin to the clock definition point in the design | create_clock –period 10 –waveform {0 5} [get_ports TCK]
